rationale: Maps the four canonical TSIR boundary probe points to the per-kernel dispatch outputs that materialize each boundary's activation tensor in the steps-mode 31B host plan (bench/out/r3-1-31b-manifest-fullgraph-compile-steps/host-plan.json). Choices: (a) post_rmsnorm → ple_rmsnorm.output (the layer's pre-attention RMS-normalized hidden state); (b) post_qkv → ple_proj.output (Q/K/V projection output before split); (c) post_attn → attn_decode.output (attention out before residual+output projection — the decode-time attention kernel; sliding/full variants share the same output symbol); (d) post_ffn → gelu_decode.output (gated MLP output after activation, before final residual). The kernel names follow the steps-mode 31B compileTargets list. If the host plan introduces alternate kernels for a probe point, override via --probe-map at the call site.

post_qkv.note: Q/K/V projection output before per-head split.

post_attn.note: Attention-block output before residual + output projection.

post_ffn.note: Gated-MLP output after activation, before final residual. The host plan compileTargets list has a single shared 'gelu' kernel (no _decode / _prefill split) per bench/out/r3-1-31b-manifest-fullgraph-compile-steps/host-plan.json.
